com.mentor.chs.api

Interface IXModuledDesign

  • All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXHarnessDesign, IXLogicDesign

    @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXModuledDesign
    Those designs that can have module codes assigned to them will extend from this interface.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode> getApplicableFunctionalModuleCodes()
      Get list of functional module codes assigned to this design.
      java.util.Set<IXProductionModuleCode> getApplicableProductionModuleCodes()
      Get list of production module codes assigned to this design.
      java.util.Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode> getAvailableFunctionalModuleCodes()
      Get list of functional module codes that are available to be assigned to the design and the objects within the design.
      java.util.Set<IXProductionModuleCode> getAvailableProductionModuleCodes()
      Get list of production module codes that are available to be assigned to the design and the objects within the design.
      java.util.Set<java.util.Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode>> getFunctionalModuleCodeCombinations()
      Gets a list of all the combinations defined for the the functional module code manager appropriate to this design
      java.util.Set<java.util.Set<IXProductionModuleCode>> getProductionModuleCodeCombinations()
      Gets a list of all the combinations defined for the the production module code manager appropriate to this design

    • Method Detail

      • getApplicableFunctionalModuleCodes

        java.util.Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode> getApplicableFunctionalModuleCodes()
        Get list of functional module codes assigned to this design.

        This is usually a subset of getAvailableFunctionalModuleCodes().

        Returns:
        Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode> Set of functional module codes assigned to this design.

      • getApplicableProductionModuleCodes

        java.util.Set<IXProductionModuleCode> getApplicableProductionModuleCodes()
        Get list of production module codes assigned to this design.

        This is usually a subset of getAvailableProductionModuleCodes().

        Returns:
        Set<IXProductionModuleCode> Set of production module codes assigned to this design.

      • getAvailableFunctionalModuleCodes

        java.util.Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode> getAvailableFunctionalModuleCodes()
        Get list of functional module codes that are available to be assigned to the design and the objects within the design.

        This is usually a super-set of getApplicableFunctionalModuleCodes().

        When assigning mew codes to objects in this design, those codes MUST be obtained from this method.

        Returns:
        Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode> Set of all functional module codes.

      • getAvailableProductionModuleCodes

        java.util.Set<IXProductionModuleCode> getAvailableProductionModuleCodes()
        Get list of production module codes that are available to be assigned to the design and the objects within the design.

        This is usually a super-set of getApplicableProductionModuleCodes().

        When assigning mew codes to objects in this design, those codes MUST be obtained from this method.

        Returns:
        Set<IXProductionModuleCode> Set of all production module codes.

      • getFunctionalModuleCodeCombinations

        java.util.Set<java.util.Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode>> getFunctionalModuleCodeCombinations()
        Gets a list of all the combinations defined for the the functional module code manager appropriate to this design
        Returns:
        Set<Set<IXProductionModuleCode>> a set of combinations where each combination is a set of functional module codes

      • getProductionModuleCodeCombinations

        java.util.Set<java.util.Set<IXProductionModuleCode>> getProductionModuleCodeCombinations()
        Gets a list of all the combinations defined for the the production module code manager appropriate to this design
        Returns:
        Set<Set<IXProductionModuleCode>> a set of combinations where each combination is a set of production module codes
